title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS company with 100+ employees across Tel Aviv and New York, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale its GTM operational backbone. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in RevOps/Sales Ops within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration expertise, revenue analytics skills, and cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Experience working with globally distributed teams and the Tel Aviv HQ is essential.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment: Senior RevOps executive with 15+ years B2B SaaS experience exceeds the 7+ year requirement, Fintech background matches cloud cost management space, Salesforce expertise and scaling experience with distributed teams directly address core needs. Tel Aviv hybrid location is ideal for candidate.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of RevOps opening and thought of you immediately—your track record scaling GTM operations in B2B SaaS and Fintech experience is exactly what they're building toward. The role reports to the CRO at a 100+ person company with Tel Aviv roots and a hybrid setup that seems built for someone who can operate seamlessly across US and Israeli teams. Given your depth with Salesforce administration and revenue analytics at scale, this feels like a natural next chapter.

title: Lead GTM Engineer
description: Engine, a fast-growing business travel and spend management platform serving 20,000+ companies, is building a Growth Engineering function from the ground up. The Lead GTM Engineer will own systems, signals, and automations that help GTM teams reach the right companies. Requirements include building/maintaining production software in growth/marketing/sales-adjacent environments, strong Python proficiency (Node.js a plus), experience integrating GTM systems like Salesforce and Outreach, comfort building internal tools and data-driven workflows, and people leadership readiness.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM systems, Salesforce integration expertise, and building data-driven workflows for revenue teams. Slight gap on Python/Node.js technical depth, though RevOps background provides foundational systems thinking needed for the role.
ai_opener: Engine's mission to help 20K+ companies optimize spend management resonates—I've spent 15+ years building the GTM infrastructure that powers revenue teams at scale. I'm particularly drawn to building from scratch the systems and signals that connect Salesforce, Outreach, and marketing automation into a cohesive growth engine, which directly mirrors my work architecting pipeline forecasting and audience segmentation workflows.

title: Staff GTM Engineer (AI & Automation)
description: Grafana Labs, a remote-first open-source observability powerhouse, seeks a Staff GTM Engineer to build next-generation AI agent and workflow systems. You'll spend most time writing code—designing multi-agent architectures, integrating LLM APIs, building data pipelines, and shipping automation across Marketing, RevOps, and SDR teams. Requires hands-on LLM framework experience (prompt engineering, RAG, agent orchestration), fluency with AI-assisted development tools, Salesforce/CRM integration experience, and at least one marketing automation platform. Ideal for builders at the intersection of AI, automation, and business growth.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM automation, RevOps, and Salesforce/marketing platform expertise. However, this is heavily IC/engineering-focused (multi-agent architectures, LLM frameworks, code-heavy) rather than leadership/strategy—better suited for a technical builder than a senior executive managing teams and revenue strategy.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years building GTM engines in B2B SaaS. Your Staff GTM Engineer role caught my attention: I've architected RevOps automation stacks across Salesforce and marketing platforms, and I've shipped AI-native go-to-market systems that actually move pipeline. I'm curious whether you're looking for someone who can architect *and* lead, or pure technical execution on the agent/automation side.

title: GTM Engineer, Revenue Operations
description: Remote, a globally distributed company, seeks a GTM Engineer to design, manage, and scale its GTM technology stack, directly influencing sales efficiency and quota achievement. You'll work cross-functionally with Sales Leadership and executives on pricing, packaging, sales processes, and methodology. Requires deep hands-on experience with GTM technology stacks, lead management and multi-touch attribution, SQL and scripting (Python/JavaScript) proficiency preferred, API integration experience, and data modeling. Reports to the Director of Revenue Systems & Processes. Base salary range $40,900–$92,050 USD.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with GTM Engineer role requiring RevOps expertise, technology stack management, and cross-functional leadership. Strong match on Omri's core competencies in sales efficiency, pipeline forecasting, and technical implementation—though the salary range ($40,900–$92,050) appears potentially below market for a 15+ year executive.
ai_opener: I've spent 15+ years scaling GTM operations for B2B SaaS companies, and your GTM Engineer role caught my attention—the focus on designing revenue tech stacks and driving sales efficiency directly aligns with how I've built and optimized Salesforce/HubSpot ecosystems that improved quota attainment. I'm particularly interested in how Remote approaches multi-touch attribution and lead management across your distributed team, and I'd welcome exploring how my experience with API integrations and data modeling could accelerate your GTM technology strategy.

title: GTM Engineer (EU Remote)
description: Unframe is hiring a GTM Engineer to build the systems powering its GTM motion and enterprise sales execution. You'll design and implement the automation, tooling, and data infrastructure that turns GTM strategy into repeatable pipeline, working directly with Sales Leadership, Enablement, Marketing, and Solutions to remove friction and scale outbound thoughtfully. The role offers real ownership from day one, building alongside founders and VPs in a fast-moving environment. Ideal for someone who enjoys creating systems that make sales teams materially more effective.

title: GTM/Growth Engineer
description: Gather AI is hiring its first Growth Engineer to join the Marketing team in a role straddling software engineering, marketing, and GTM strategy. You'll own the GTM data stack, build AI-powered tools (AI-assisted lead scoring, automated sales summaries, interactive demos), instrument data flow between product, website, and GTM systems (Salesforce, HubSpot), and run A/B experiments to optimize conversion. This is a builder's role reporting to Marketing with a dotted line to Engineering, where your code directly impacts revenue.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, RevOps stack expertise (Salesforce/HubSpot), and AI-native GTM strategy. Primary gap: role emphasizes software engineering/coding skills rather than pure operations, and reporting to Marketing vs. Revenue leadership is non-standard for Omri's seniority level.
ai_opener: Gather AI is solving a real pain point—most GTM teams are drowning in disconnected data across Salesforce, HubSpot, and their website. As someone who's spent 15+ years architecting GTM data stacks and optimizing pipeline forecasting for B2B SaaS, I'm intrigued by your approach to AI-powered lead scoring and automated sales workflows. I'd be curious whether you're looking for someone who can both engineer the infrastructure and drive the GTM strategy it powers.
